content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5602         322-5660            322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

August 13, 1982


Mr. Steven M. Rizzuto
Treasurer, City of Atascadero
7109 El Camino Real
Atascadero, CA  93422-9990

Re: Your Request for Advice
     Our No. A-82-124

Dear Mr. Rizzuto:

     Thank you for your request for advice from this office.
In your letter, you stated that you are the newly-elected
treasurer for the City of Atascadero. You are also employed
by Fidelity Federal Savings of San Francisco ("Fidelity
Savings"), an institution with which the City places some of
its funds. Your question is whether there is a conflict of
interest in your holding both positions.

FACTS

     In your position as Treasurer for the City of Atascadero,
you determine active and inactive reserves of public funds and
securely place the reserves at appropriate interest rates. You
plan to continue the method that has been used in the past for
placing funds, which involves placing deposits with local banks
and savings and loans based upon a competitive bidding system
that uses rate of return as a determining factor. Your employer,
Fidelity Savings, is one of the financial institutions which is
regularly used.

     In your employment with Fidelity Savings, you are an
Assistant Vice-President and Central Coast District Supervisor.
Your responsibilities are broad and include supervising seven
branch savings office managers, the district operating plan and
budget variances, marketing programs, and the business develop-
ment activities of branch managers in their marketing areas.
You do not set interest rates, nor are you involved in develop-
ment decisions on interest rates.

Mr. Steven M. Rizzuto
August 13, 1982
Page Two


Your review of Atascadero's inactive reserve portfolio
revealed that, as Treasurer, you are responsible for a varying
portfolio of approximately one million dollars. The total
portfolio exists as separate deposit certificates with values
of between $100,000 and $250,000, currently divided between
approximately six local financial institutions.

DISCUSSION

The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from acting
as Treasurer while you are employed by Fidelity Savings.1/
Rather, the Act affects your decision-making abilities as
Treasurer by requiring you to disqualify yourself from making
or participating in decisions which you have a conflict of
interest.

Government Code Section 871002/ provides:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his official position to influence
a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interest.

An official has a "financial interest" in a decision within
the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect on any
source of income aggregating $250 or more received by the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision
is made.

In the present situation, Fidelity Savings is a source of
income to you. As Treasurer, any decision you are confronted
with that will increase Fidelity Savings' current liabilities
by $100,000 or more is a decision that will have a material

___________________

1/ However, you should consult the City Attorney or the
Attorney General's Office regarding the possible application
of Government Code Sections 1090, et seq.

2/ Hereinafter all statutory references made are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

Mr. Steven M. Rizzuto
August 13, 1982
Page Three


financial effect on the savings and loan and will require your
disqualification.  (2 California Administrative Code Section
18702(b)(3)(C).)  A decision to place even one of Abesadero's
certificates of deposit with Fidelity Savings would be such a
decision, for the smallest certificate is worth $100,000.

The use of a competitive bidding system may affect the issue
of whether or not you are making, or participating in the making
of, a decision to place money with Fidelity Savings.  If this
bidding system removes all of your involvement in determining
whether Fidelity Savings is chosen as one of the institutions
to be used and, if your involvement in any contractual arrange-
ments with Fidelity Savings is purely ministerial, i.e., you
merely sign a contract and cannot alter the contract terms, then
you are not making, or participating in the making of, a decision
within the meaning of the Act.  (2 California Administrative
Code Section 18700(d).)

However, if you can affect the decision as to whether Fidelity
Savings is chosen, or, if you have the discretion to negotiate
contract terms with Fidelity Savings, or, if you advise or make
recommendations regarding whether or not to place funds with
Fidelity Savings, then you are impermissibly making and partici-
pating in the making of a decision which will have a material
financial effect on Fidelity Savings.  (2 California Administra-
tive Code Section 18700(b) and (c).)

However, I would like to mention to you the provisions of
Section 87101.  This states that a public official is not pre-
vented from making, or participating in the making of, a decision
to the extent his participation is legally required for the deci-
sion to be made.  An official is legally required to participate
only where there exists no alternative source of decision con-
sistent with the purposes and terms of the statute authorizing
the decision.  (See 2 California Administrative Code Section
18701(a), copy enclosed.)

I assume that there exists someone who can act in your
place in situations where you are disqualified.  If there is not,
the provisions of Section 87101 and 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18701 may be applicable.  If you feel that these
provisions may apply, please contact me so that we can further
explore the facts and the possible application of these sections
to your situation.

Mr. Steven M. Rizzuto
August 13, 1982
Page Four


     I would also like to note that, even though it appears
that you will be required to abstain from making, or partici-
pating in the making of, decisions materially affecting Fidelity
Savings, absent any other conflict of interests, you are free
to consider the placement of funds with other financial
institutions.3/

     If you have any additional questions, please feel free
to contact me at 916/322-5901.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   Janis Shank McLean
                                   Counsel
                                   Legal Division

JSM:km
Enclosures




_______________________

     3/  So long as the reasonably foreseeable effects of such
activities will not materially affect Fidelity Savings.

Fidelity Savings
and Loan Association
of San Francisco
A FEDERAL SAVINGS AND LOAN ASSOCIATION

June 30, 1982

Janis Shank McLean, Counsel
Legal Division, Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

re: Request for Determination

Dear Counselor:

I have received the Political Reform Act of 1974 (amended) publication and
a copy of California Administrative Code section 18,702. I request a
"conflict of interest" determination based upon the following information.

I have recently been elected as Treasurer for the general law city of
Atascadero, California. Pursuant to government code and city ordinance,
my primary responsibilities as City Treasurer will be to determine active
and inactive reserves of public funds and place those reserves securely
and at interest as I determine to be in the best public interest.

Our city was incorporated on July 2, 1979. During this time, the Director
of Finance has fulfilled the statutory requirements placed upon the city
treasurer. During his tenure, the services of local banks and savings
and loan associations were called upon as depositories of inactive
reserves of the city. The deposits were awarded to a great degree on
a 'bid' type format, using rate of return as a determining factor.
Included in the list of regularly utilized savings institutions is
Fidelity Federal Savings of San Francisco, dba Fidelity Savings of
San Francisco.

I am currently an employee of Fidelity Savings and hold the title of
Assistant Vice President and Central Coast District Supervisor. Acting
in this capacity, I am primarily responsible for the supervision of
seven branch savings office managers. My responsibilities are operationally
oriented and broad; I am responsible for the district operating plan
and budget variances, marketing programs as delegated by our Marketing
Department Vice President, personnel maintenance, and business development activities of our branch savings managers in their respective market
areas. At my level of supervision, I do not participate in daily customer
transactions but rather intercede in situations where policy interpretation
or application practice may be required to insure a proper level of customer
service. I do not establish interest rates on customer deposits nor do
I assist in the development decision for interest rates; interest rates
are either regularly set or, in the case of negotiable certificates of
deposits, established by our Corporate Treasurer. The corporate treasurer

7109 EL CAMINO REAL • ATASCADERO, CALIFORNIA 93422-9990 • 805-466-6550
Board of Directors: Harold R. Walt, Father Timothy L. McDonnell, S.J. J. Ralph Stone, Edward H. Barker, Sherman Miller

page 2.

works directly through the servicing branch manager. The Association holds
liabilities in the form of customer deposits in excess of one billion dollars.
The district for which I am responsible has similar liabilities in the amount
of approximately fifty-five million dollars.

After careful review of the Atascadero Inactive reserve portfolio, I would
anticipate being responsible for a varying porfolio of approximately one
million dollars. The total portfolio exists as seperate deposit certificates
with value between $100,000 - $250,000, currently divided between approximately
six financial institutions in the community.

The method of identifying and depositing with certain depositaries currently
in existence will be perpetuated, that is to say, we will continue with
the opportunity to instigate competitive bidding on deposit contracts and,
award deposits that exist in the best public interest.

I request a determination from the Legal Division to determine whether my
employment interest with Fidelity Savings of San Francisco is material with
effect on my daily responsibilities as the Atascadero City Treasurer. I
am willing to submit further information if requested.

Very respectfully submitted,

[signature]

Steven M. Rizzuto, Treasurer
City of Atascadero, California

cc  Alan Grimes, City Attorney
    Rolfe Nelson, Mayor, City of Atascadero

Attach to existing
letter

ADVICE FILE SUMMARY

TYPE: M                                    DATE: Aug 31, 1982

YEAR: 1982                                 STAFF: [illegible]

NUMBER: 124                                ADVISEE: Rizzuto

AGENCY: City Treasurer, Piacadero

CODE SEC/REG NO.: 89100
                  18101
                  89101

Attach to existing
SUMMARY: letter


SEC#: ________    DON'T PRINT: ____   SEC RE#D: ____   LAW CHANGED: ____
      ________                  ____              ____              ____
      ________                  ____              ____              ____

BULLETIN SUMMARY: _________________________________________________

As to those decisions over which Rizzuto exercised
discretion, regarding the placement of funds w/ Fidelity,
he must disqualify himself.

The provisions of 87101 ("legally required participation") do not
apply. The city provision allow the treasurer to appoint
a deputy. The state provides that the treasurer
and his/her bondsman remain responsible
for the deputy's actions. The fact that they are "reasonable"
& doesn't make the treasurers participation mandatory.
The responsibility is minor due to tort immunity
and criminal liability protection (bond). His responsibility
is primarily that of picking an appropriate deputy

sections.question: Your question is whether there is a conflict of interest in your holding both positions.
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from acting as Treasurer while you are employed by Fidelity Savings. Rather, the Act affects your decision-making abilities as Treasurer by requiring you to disqualify yourself from making or participating in decisions which you have a conflict of interest. A decision to place even one of Atascadero's certificates of deposit with Fidelity Savings would require your disqualification, as the smallest certificate is worth $100,000 and would have a material financial effect. However, if a competitive bidding system removes all your involvement in determining whether Fidelity Savings is chosen and your involvement is purely ministerial, you would not be making or participating in a prohibited decision.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Steven M. Rizzuto, as newly-elected Treasurer of the City of Atascadero, has a conflict of interest while employed by Fidelity Federal Savings of San Francisco, an institution where the city places municipal funds.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that Rizzuto must disqualify himself from decisions placing city funds with Fidelity Savings due to material financial interest, but may participate in decisions regarding other institutions. Disqualification requirements depend on whether the competitive bidding system removes his discretionary involvement in selecting Fidelity Savings or negotiating contract terms.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated June 30, 1982) and the FPPC response letter (dated August 13, 1982). Question and conclusion sections are clearly identified. Reference to Government Code Sections 87100, 87101 and California Administrative Code Section 18702. Handwritten note at bottom indicates file summary form.

QUESTION: Your question is whether there is a conflict of interest in your holding both positions.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from acting as Treasurer while you are employed by Fidelity Savings. Rather, the Act affects your decision-making abilities as Treasurer by requiring you to disqualify yourself from making or participating in decisions which you have a conflict of interest. A decision to place even one of Atascadero's certificates of deposit with Fidelity Savings would require your disqualification, as the smallest certificate is worth $100,000 and would have a material financial effect. However, if a competitive bidding system removes all your involvement in determining whether Fidelity Savings is chosen and your involvement is purely ministerial, you would not be making or participating in a prohibited decision.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised Steven M. Rizzuto that while serving as City Treasurer of Atascadero, he must disqualify himself from decisions regarding deposit placement with his employer Fidelity Federal Savings, as such decisions would materially affect his source of income. The FPPC clarified that disqualification is required only if Rizzuto has discretionary involvement; a purely ministerial role under competitive bidding may permit participation.
